sourceText: Shoqan Walikhanov
translatedText: Shoqan Walikhanov

sourceText: A people's memory is their history.
translatedText: Tunawa da mutane shine tarihinsu.

sourceText: The lonely struggle to preserve the identity and culture of my people amidst Russian imperial expansion, all while battling frail health which led to a tragically short life.
translatedText: Gwagwarmayar kadaitacciya don adana asali da al'adun mutanena a tsakanin fadada daular Rasha, duk yayin da ake fama da rashin lafiya wanda ya haifar da gajeriyar rayuwa.

sourceText: I leveraged my unique position as a Russian-educated Kazakh noble and officer to scientifically document and study the history, languages, and traditions of Central Asian peoples, giving them a voice in the world of scholarship.
translatedText: Na yi amfani da matsayina na musamman a matsayina na ɗan Kazakh mai ilimi na Rasha kuma jami'i don yin rubuce-rubuce da nazarin tarihi, harsuna, da al'adun mutanen tsakiyar Asiya, tare da ba su murya a cikin duniyar malanta.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Facing the political and religious turmoil of the Iconoclast controversy and forgoing a potential life as empress for one of spiritual devotion.
translatedText: Fuskantar rikice-rikicen siyasa da na addini na rigimar Iconoclast da barin rayuwa mai yuwuwa a matsayin ɗaukaka ga ɗayan ibada na ruhaniya.

sourceText: She founded a convent, dedicating her life to God and channeling her intellect and artistry into creating a lasting legacy of hymns and poetry.
translatedText: Ta kafa gidan zuhudu, inda ta sadaukar da rayuwarta ga Allah tare da ba da hankali da fasaha wajen samar da dawwamammen tarihi na wakoki da wakoki.

sourceText: 9th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine na Karni na 9

sourceText: Constantine XI Palaiologos
translatedText: Constantine XI Palaiologos

sourceText: To surrender the city to you is beyond my authority... We have all decided to die with our own free will.
translatedText: In mika muku birnin a hannuna ya fi karfina... Dukkanmu mun yanke shawarar mutuwa ne da son ranmu.

sourceText: The profound isolation of leading a dying empire, betrayed by the divisions of Christendom and facing an overwhelming enemy.
translatedText: Keɓance mai zurfi na jagorancin daular da ke mutuwa, rarrabuwar kawuna na Kiristendam ya ci amana kuma yana fuskantar babban maƙiyi.

sourceText: By choosing to die in battle alongside his soldiers and citizens, he transformed a military defeat into a legendary act of martyrdom and imperial dignity.
translatedText: Ta wurin zaɓen ya mutu a yaƙi tare da sojojinsa da ƴan ƙasa, ya sāke shan kashi na soja ya zama abin almara na shahada da mutuncin sarki.

sourceText: Mid-15th Century
translatedText: Tsakiyar Karni na 15

sourceText: Kublai Khan
translatedText: Kublai Khan

sourceText: I have conquered the world on horseback, but I cannot rule it from horseback.
translatedText: Na ci duniya a kan doki, amma ba zan iya yin mulkinta da doki ba.

sourceText: Constant succession struggles with my own kin, the costly and difficult conquest of the Southern Song, and the devastating failures of my naval expeditions against Japan.
translatedText: Rikici na yau da kullun yana gwagwarmaya tare da dangi na, nasara mai tsada da wahala na waƙar Kudancin, da mummunan gazawar balaguron ruwa na da Japan.

sourceText: By adopting Chinese administrative systems, promoting trade and infrastructure like the Grand Canal, and embracing a policy of religious tolerance, I consolidated my rule and created a prosperous, multicultural empire.
translatedText: Ta hanyar yin amfani da tsarin gudanarwa na kasar Sin, da inganta kasuwanci da ababen more rayuwa kamar babban kogin ruwa, da kuma rungumar manufar yin hakuri da addini, na karfafa mulkina, na samar da daula mai wadata, da al'adu daban-daban.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: To what a barbarous country have you sent me; the dwellings are gloomy, the churches ugly, and the customs revolting.
translatedText: To wace kasa ce ka aike ni; Gidajen sun yi duhu, Ikklisiya sun yi muni, al'adu kuma suna tayarwa.

sourceText: Cultural shock and loneliness in a foreign land, and the political intrigues she faced as regent after her husband's death.
translatedText: Rikicin al'ada da kadaici a wata ƙasa, da kuma irin makircin siyasa da ta fuskanta a matsayinta na mai mulki bayan mutuwar mijinta.

sourceText: Used her superior education and intellect to actively participate in French politics, securing her son Philip I's throne and proving her influence.
translatedText: Ta yi amfani da iliminta mafi girma da hankali don shiga cikin siyasa ta Faransa, ta sami nasarar danta Philip I a gadon sarauta tare da tabbatar da tasirinta.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11

sourceText: Olga of Kyiv
translatedText: Olga na Kyiv

sourceText: What begins with fire shall end in ashes.
translatedText: Abin da ya fara da wuta zai ƙare da toka.

sourceText: The brutal murder of her husband, Igor of Kyiv, by the Drevlian tribe.
translatedText: Kisan gillar da kabilar Drevlian ta yi wa mijinta, Igor na Kyiv.

sourceText: Securing her rule through a series of calculated and brutal acts of revenge, followed by significant administrative reforms and her eventual conversion to Christianity.
translatedText: Tabbatar da mulkinta ta hanyar jerin ayyukan ƙididdigewa da zalunci na ramuwar gayya, tare da gagarumin gyare-gyaren gudanarwa da kuma tubarta daga ƙarshe zuwa Kiristanci.

sourceText: 10th Century
translatedText: Karni na 10

sourceText: Taras Shevchenko
translatedText: Taras Shevchenko

sourceText: Fight, and you will overcome. God will help you!
translatedText: Ku yi yaƙi, kuma za ku yi nasara. Allah zai taimake ku!

sourceText: Born into serfdom, years of exile, and a strict ban on writing and painting.
translatedText: An haife shi a cikin bautar gumaka, shekarun gudun hijira, da tsauraran dokar hana rubutu da zane.

sourceText: Redeemed from serfdom by friends, he continued to write in secret during exile, becoming a symbol of Ukrainian resilience.
translatedText: An fanshe shi daga saɓo daga abokai, ya ci gaba da rubutawa a asirce lokacin gudun hijira, ya zama alama ce ta juriyar Yukren.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Tamar of Georgia
translatedText: Tamar Jojiya

sourceText: I have nursed a lion, but it has turned on me.
translatedText: Na shayar da zaki, amma ya juya mini.

sourceText: Facing opposition from nobles who questioned a woman's right to rule, and enduring a tumultuous first marriage that ended in a coup attempt.
translatedText: Fuskantar adawa daga manyan mutane da suka koka game da 'yancin mace na mulki, da kuma jure wa auren farko da aka yi na tashin hankali wanda ya ƙare a yunkurin juyin mulki.

sourceText: Asserting her authority with political acumen, leading her armies to victory, and forging a successful second marriage to secure her dynasty and kingdom.
translatedText: Tabbatar da ikonta da basirar siyasa, ya jagoranci sojojinta zuwa ga nasara, da yin nasarar aure na biyu don tabbatar da daularta da mulkinta.

sourceText: 12th-13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12-13

sourceText: Royalty is a fine burial shroud.
translatedText: Sarauta rigar kabari ce mai kyau.

sourceText: Enduring scorn for my humble origins as an actress and facing constant threats from political rivals within the court.
translatedText: Daurewa raini ga asali na tawali'u a matsayina na 'yar wasan kwaikwayo da kuma fuskantar barazana daga abokan hamayyar siyasa a cikin kotu.

sourceText: By earning the absolute trust and love of Emperor Justinian, I proved my intelligence and resolve, establishing myself as his co-ruler and most trusted advisor.
translatedText: Ta hanyar samun cikakkiyar amana da ƙaunar Sarkin sarakuna Justinian, na tabbatar da hankalina da azama, na kafa kaina a matsayin abokin tafiyarsa kuma amintaccen mashawarcinsa.

sourceText: 6th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine na Karni na 6

sourceText: Theophanes the Greek
translatedText: Theophanes Girkanci

sourceText: While he was painting... no one ever saw him looking at models... he seemed to be painting with his hands, while his mind was contemplating something lofty and wise.
translatedText: A lokacin da yake zane...babu wanda ya taba ganinsa yana kallon model...da alama ya zana da hannunsa,yayinda hankalinsa yana tunanin wani abu mai daukaka da hikima.

sourceText: Life as an itinerant artist, leaving his Byzantine homeland to work in foreign lands, and the artistic struggle to forge a unique, expressive style within the strict conventions of icon painting.
translatedText: Rayuwa a matsayin mai zane-zane mai tafiya, yana barin mahaifarsa ta Byzantine don yin aiki a ƙasashen waje, da gwagwarmayar fasaha don ƙirƙirar salo na musamman, bayyananne a cikin tsauraran ƙa'idodi na zanen gumaka.

sourceText: Achieving great fame and patronage across the Russian principalities, creating masterpieces that defined an era, and securing his legacy by mentoring the next generation of masters, like Andrei Rublev.
translatedText: Achieving great fame and patronage across the Russian principalities, creating masterpieces that defined an era, and securing his legacy by mentoring the next generation of masters, like Andrei Rublev.

sourceText: Late 14th - Early 15th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 14th - Farkon Ƙarni na 15

sourceText: I did not sleep at night, I did not rest at day. I served, shedding my red blood and my black sweat.
translatedText: Ban yi barci da dare ba, ban huta da rana ba. Na yi hidima, na zubar da jajayen jinina da bakar gumi na.

sourceText: Witnessing the subjugation and dispersal of the Turkic people under the rule of the Tang Dynasty.
translatedText: Shaidar da aka yi da kuma tarwatsa mutanen Turkawa a karkashin mulkin Daular Tang.

sourceText: By persuading Ilterish Khagan to revolt and using his military and diplomatic genius to establish and stabilize the Second Turkic Khaganate.
translatedText: Ta hanyar lallashin Ilterish Khagan ya yi tawaye da yin amfani da hazakar soja da diflomasiyya don kafa da daidaita Turkawa Khaganate na biyu.

sourceText: 7th-8th Century
translatedText: Karni na 7-8

sourceText: Timur (Tamerlane)
translatedText: Timur (Tamerlane)

sourceText: The whole expanse of the inhabited part of the world is not large enough to have two kings.
translatedText: Duk faɗin yankin duniya bai isa ya sami sarakuna biyu ba.

sourceText: Suffered severe injuries in his youth that left him with a permanent limp, leading to the Persian nickname 'Timur-i-Lang' (Timur the Lame). He also lacked a direct bloodline to Genghis Khan, a crucial source of legitimacy.
translatedText: Ya sha fama da munanan raunuka a lokacin ƙuruciyarsa wanda ya bar shi da gurguwa na dindindin, wanda ya kai ga laƙabin Farisa 'Timur-i-Lang' (Timur the Lame). Ya kuma rasa layin jini kai tsaye ga Genghis Khan, mahimmin tushen halal.

sourceText: He compensated for his physical disability with immense willpower and strategic brilliance. To bolster his legitimacy, he married a princess of Chagatai-Khanate, a descendant of Genghis Khan, adopting the title 'Guregen' (royal son-in-law), and styled himself as a champion of Islam.
translatedText: Ya rama nakasawarsa ta jiki tare da ƙwaƙƙwaran ƙarfi da hazaka na dabara. Don ƙarfafa haƙƙinsa, ya auri wata gimbiya Chagatai-Khanate, zuriyar Genghis Khan, inda ya ɗauki lakabin 'Guregen' ( surukin sarki), kuma ya sanya kansa a matsayin gwarzon Musulunci.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14

sourceText: Petro Mohyla
translatedText: Petro Mohyla

sourceText: Our faith must be fortified by education and reason.
translatedText: Dole ne a ƙarfafa bangaskiyarmu ta ilimi da hankali.

sourceText: The immense pressure to restore the identity of a divided Orthodox Church, caught between the influences of Catholicism and Protestantism.
translatedText: Babban matsi na maido da asalin Cocin Orthodox da aka raba, wanda aka kama tsakanin tasirin Katolika da Furotesta.

sourceText: By compiling the 'Orthodox Confession of Faith' to systematize doctrine and by founding the Mohyla Academy to cultivate a new generation of clergy and scholars.
translatedText: Ta hanyar tattara 'Confession of Faith Orthodox' don tsara koyaswa da kuma kafa Mohyla Academy don haɓaka sabon ƙarni na malamai da malamai.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky
translatedText: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky

sourceText: To regret the past, to hope in the future, and never to be satisfied with the present: that is my life.
translatedText: Don yin nadama a baya, don bege nan gaba, kuma kada in gamsu da halin yanzu: wannan shine rayuwata.

sourceText: Lifelong depression, societal prejudice and internal conflict, a tragic marriage.
translatedText: Bacin rai na rayuwa, son zuciya da rikice-rikice na cikin gida, aure mai ban tausayi.

sourceText: Emotional sublimation through music, financial and emotional support from his patron Nadezhda von Meck.
translatedText: Ƙaƙwalwar motsin rai ta hanyar kiɗa, tallafi na kuɗi da motsin rai daga majiɓincin sa Nadezhda von Meck.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: I have written these things in this way, in order that the events of the past may not be completely lost in the abyss of time...
translatedText: Na rubuta wadannan abubuwa ne ta wannan hanya, domin kada al’amuran da suka faru a baya su lalace gaba daya a cikin matsi na zamani...

sourceText: Witnessing the immense suffering caused by constant warfare and the moral corruption of the imperial court.
translatedText: Shaidu da gaggarumin wahala da yaƙe-yaƙe na yau da kullun suka haifar da lalata ɗabi'a na kotun daular.

sourceText: He channeled his disillusionment into his writing, creating a public history to praise the emperor's successes and a secret history to expose the regime's dark side.
translatedText: Ya sanya bacin ransa a cikin rubuce-rubucensa, inda ya kirkiro tarihin jama'a don yaba nasarorin da sarki ya samu da kuma tarihin sirri don fallasa barakar gwamnatin.

sourceText: 6th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine na Karni na 6

sourceText: Not to us, Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory.
translatedText: Ba a gare mu ba, Ubangiji, ba mu ba, amma sunanka ya ɗaukaka.

sourceText: After decades of war to save the empire from the Persians, I had to watch helplessly as the holy lands of Syria, Palestine, and Egypt fell to the new Arab armies.
translatedText: Bayan yaƙe-yaƙe na shekaru da yawa don ceton daular daga Farisa, dole ne in yi kallo ba tare da taimako ba yayin da ƙasashe masu tsarki na Siriya, Falasdinu, da Masar suka faɗa hannun sababbin sojojin Larabawa.

sourceText: I personally led my armies, employing bold strategies to achieve a decisive victory over Persia. I also reformed the imperial administration and military, establishing the Theme system that would be the backbone of the Empire for centuries.
translatedText: Ni da kaina na ja-goranci sojojina, na yi amfani da dabarun da suka dace don in ci nasara a kan Farisa. Na kuma gyara tsarin mulki da sojoji, na kafa tsarin Jigo wanda zai zama kashin bayan Daular tsawon shekaru aru-aru.

sourceText: 7th Century
translatedText: Karni na 7

sourceText: All things are but manifestations of the mind; in truth, there is no dharma outside the mind.
translatedText: Ba komai ba face bayyanar hankali; a gaskiya, babu wani darma a waje da hankali.

sourceText: The frustration with contradictory and incomplete Buddhist texts in China, and the perilous, unsanctioned journey across deserts and mountains.
translatedText: Bacin rai tare da sabani da nassosin addinin Buddah a kasar Sin, da bala'in bala'i, mara izini a hamada da tsaunuka.

sourceText: Successfully returned with over 650 Buddhist texts, founded the Yogacara school in China, and left a detailed record of his travels, influencing cultures for centuries.
translatedText: An dawo da nasara tare da rubutun addinin Buddha sama da 650, ya kafa makarantar Yogacara a kasar Sin, kuma ya bar cikakken tarihin tafiye-tafiyensa, yana tasiri al'adu tsawon ƙarni.

sourceText: 7th Century Tang Dynasty
translatedText: Daular Tang ta Karni na 7

sourceText: Hryhorii Skovoroda
translatedText: Hryhorii Skovoroda

sourceText: The world tried to catch me, but it couldn't.
translatedText: Duniya tayi kokarin kama ni, amma ta kasa.

sourceText: Rejection of worldly success, institutional constraints, and a life of constant wandering.
translatedText: ƙin cin nasara na duniya, matsalolin hukumomi, da rayuwar yawo akai-akai.

sourceText: Found true happiness not in possessions or status, but through a philosophy of inner freedom and self-knowledge.
translatedText: An sami farin ciki na gaske ba a cikin dukiya ko matsayi ba, amma ta hanyar falsafar 'yanci na ciki da sanin kai.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Gertrude Bell
translatedText: Gertrude Bell

sourceText: To be able to laugh and to be merciful are the only things that make man better than the beast.
translatedText: Don iya dariya da jin ƙai su ne kawai abubuwan da ke sa mutum ya fi dabba.

sourceText: Navigating a male-dominated world of politics and exploration, and suffering personal heartbreaks, including the deaths of two men she loved.
translatedText: Kewaya duniyar siyasa da bincike da maza suka mamaye, da kuma fama da baƙin ciki, gami da mutuwar maza biyu da take ƙauna.

sourceText: She channeled her intellect and passion into her work, earning the respect of British officials and Arab leaders alike, and leaving a lasting political and cultural legacy.
translatedText: Ta ba da hankali da sha'awarta a cikin aikinta, inda ta sami girmamawa daga jami'an Birtaniya da shugabannin Larabawa, tare da barin gado mai dorewa na siyasa da al'adu.

sourceText: Late 19th and Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th da Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: A kingdom cannot exist without a great army. And an army cannot be maintained without finances, and finances cannot be secured without the prosperity of the people.
translatedText: Mulki ba zai wanzu ba sai da babbar runduna. Kuma ba za a iya kula da sojoji ba tare da kudi ba, kuma ba za a iya tabbatar da kudi ba sai da wadatar jama’a.

sourceText: Constant threats of rebellion, betrayal from those closest to me, and the paranoia that consumed my later years.
translatedText: Barazanar tawaye na dindindin, cin amana daga na kusa da ni, da rugujewar da ta cinye shekarun baya.

sourceText: Through decisive military victories and ruthless consolidation of power, I restored the Persian Empire. However, the trust I lost was never recovered.
translatedText: Ta gagarumin nasarori na soji da kuma ƙarfafa iko na rashin tausayi, na maido da Daular Farisa. Duk da haka, amincewar da na rasa ba ta sake dawowa ba.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Namık Kemal
translatedText: Namik Kemal

sourceText: A person cannot live without a fatherland.
translatedText: Mutum ba zai iya rayuwa ba tare da uba ba.

sourceText: Witnessing the decline of the Ottoman Empire under absolute monarchy and facing constant persecution, including exile and imprisonment, for my political ideas.
translatedText: Shaida ta koma bayan daular Ottoman a karkashin cikakkiyar masarauta da fuskantar tsanantawa akai-akai, gami da gudun hijira da dauri, saboda ra'ayina na siyasa.

sourceText: By continuing to write prolifically—plays, novels, and newspaper articles—even from exile, spreading the ideals of constitutionalism and patriotism to a new generation.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ci gaba da yin rubuce-rubuce masu mahimmanci-wasan kwaikwayo, litattafai, da labaran jaridu-ko da daga gudun hijira, yada manufofin tsarin mulki da kishin kasa zuwa sabon zamani.

sourceText: 19th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Daular Usmaniyya ta Karni na 19

sourceText: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi
translatedText: Nasiru Din al-Tusi

sourceText: A human being is a social animal and cannot attain perfection by himself.
translatedText: Dan Adam dabba ne na zamantakewa kuma ba zai iya samun kamala da kansa ba.

sourceText: Political turmoil and being held captive by the Assassins in their mountain fortresses.
translatedText: Rigingimun siyasa da kuma tsare su da maharan suka yi a sansanoninsu na tsaunuka.

sourceText: Gaining the patronage of the Mongol ruler Hulagu Khan to establish the Maragheh observatory, a center of scientific learning.
translatedText: Samun goyon bayan mai mulkin Mongol Hulagu Khan don kafa Maragheh Observatory, cibiyar koyon kimiyya.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: Nebuchadnezzar II
translatedText: Nebukadnezzar II

sourceText: Constant rebellions, external threats to my empire's stability, and periods of profound mental anguish, which some say were a divine judgment.
translatedText: Tawaye na yau da kullun, barazanar waje ga kwanciyar hankali na daular, da lokacin tsananin baƙin ciki, wanda wasu ke cewa hukuncin Allah ne.

sourceText: Through decisive military campaigns to crush dissent and by undertaking monumental construction projects to glorify the gods and demonstrate the power of my empire.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ƙaƙƙarfan kamfen na soji don murkushe ƴan adawa da gudanar da manyan ayyuka na gine-gine don ɗaukaka alloli da nuna ikon daulara.

sourceText: 7th-6th Century BCE
translatedText: Karni na 7-6 KZ

sourceText: Nizam al-Mulk
translatedText: Nizam al-Mulk

sourceText: Navigating the treacherous court politics, managing a vast and diverse empire, and confronting the rising threat of the Assassins (Hashshashin).
translatedText: Yawaita siyasar kotu mayaudara, sarrafa daula mai fadi da yawa, da fuskantar barazanar kashe kashe (Hashshashin).

sourceText: By creating a robust administrative bureaucracy, promoting Sunni orthodoxy through education, and authoring the 'Siyasatnama' (Book of Government) to guide future rulers.
translatedText: Ta hanyar samar da ingantaccen tsarin mulki, inganta tsarin addinin Sunna ta hanyar ilimi, da rubuta 'Siyasatnama' (Littafin Gwamnati) don jagorantar masu mulki a nan gaba.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11

sourceText: I faced the immense challenge of legitimizing my rule after seizing the throne and had to quell widespread rebellions across the empire.
translatedText: Na fuskanci ƙalubale mai girma na halasta mulki na bayan na karɓe mulki kuma na kawar da tawaye da ya yaɗu a daular.

sourceText: I asserted my divine right to rule through the Behistun Inscription, crushed the rebellions with military might, and then unified the empire through administrative genius, creating the satrapy system and the Royal Road.
translatedText: Na tabbatar da hakkina na allahntaka na yin mulki ta hanyar Rubutun Behistun, na murkushe ’yan tawaye da karfin soja, sannan na hada daular ta hanyar hazaka na gudanarwa, na samar da tsarin satrapy da Royal Road.

sourceText: 6th-5th Century BCE
translatedText: Karni na 6-5 KZ

sourceText: Rashid Rida
translatedText: Rashid Rida

sourceText: Witnessing the decline of the Ottoman Empire and the intellectual stagnation of the Muslim world under the pressure of European colonialism.
translatedText: Shaidawa tabarbarewar daular Usmaniyya da tabarbarewar tunani na duniyar musulmi karkashin matsin lambar turawan mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: By advocating for a return to the Quran and Sunnah (Salafism) and using his journal, Al-Manar, to spread reformist ideas across the Islamic world.
translatedText: Ta hanyar bayar da shawarar komawa ga Al-Qur'ani da Sunna (Salafiyya) da kuma amfani da mujallarsa ta Al-Manar wajen yada ra'ayoyin kawo sauyi a fadin duniyar Musulunci.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Foreign domination and internal fragmentation
translatedText: Mallakar kasashen waje da rarrabuwar kawuna

sourceText: Centralization of power and sweeping modernization
translatedText: Ƙaddamar da iko da haɓakar zamani

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Persecuted as a heretic by the Zoroastrian priesthood and eventually imprisoned and executed under the Sasanian emperor Bahram I.
translatedText: Firistoci na Zoroastrian sun tsananta masa a matsayin ɗan bidi'a kuma a ƙarshe an ɗaure su kuma aka kashe su a ƙarƙashin Sarkin Sasaniya Bahram I.

sourceText: Despite his martyrdom, his disciples successfully spread his teachings across the ancient world, from the Roman Empire to China.
translatedText: Duk da shahadarsa, almajiransa sun yi nasarar yada koyarwarsa a cikin duniyar da, tun daga daular Roma zuwa kasar Sin.

sourceText: 3rd Century Sasanian Empire
translatedText: Daular Sasaniya ta Karni na 3

sourceText: The relentless opposition from the conservative Janissary corps and the constant threats to the empire's integrity from both internal rebellions and foreign powers.
translatedText: Haɓaka mara kakkautawa daga ƙungiyar masu ra'ayin mazan jiya na Janissary da kuma barazanar da ake yi a kai a kai ga amincin daular daga 'yan tawayen cikin gida da na ƙasashen waje.

sourceText: By decisively dismantling the Janissaries in the 'Auspicious Incident' and establishing a new, modern army, I was able to push through my agenda of centralized reform.
translatedText: Ta hanyar tarwatsa Janissaries a cikin 'Auspicious Event' da kafa sabuwar runduna ta zamani, na sami damar ci gaba da aiwatar da tsarina na kawo sauyi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Mehmed the Conqueror
translatedText: Mehmed Mai Nasara

sourceText: Ascending to the throne at a young age amidst court intrigue and external threats, and the immense challenge of breaching the 'impenetrable' walls of Constantinople.
translatedText: Hauwa kan karagar mulki tun yana karami a cikin rugujewar kotu da barazanar waje, da kuma babban kalubalen keta bangon 'marasa tushe' na Konstantinoful.

sourceText: Through meticulous planning, the use of innovative siege technology like massive cannons, and unwavering resolve, I overcame all opposition to achieve my destiny.
translatedText: Ta hanyar tsararren tsari, amfani da sabbin fasahar kewayewa kamar manya-manyan igwa, da ƙudiri mai kauri, na shawo kan duk wata adawa don cimma makoma ta.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karni na 15

sourceText: The collapse of the Ottoman Empire, foreign occupation, and the deep-rooted resistance to the modernization my country desperately needed.
translatedText: Rugujewar daular Usmaniyya, mamayar kasashen waje, da tsayin daka na tsayin daka ga zamanantar da kasata ke matukar bukata.

sourceText: By leading the Turkish War of Independence to victory, abolishing the Sultanate, and establishing a republic. I then implemented a series of radical reforms to create a modern, secular nation-state.
translatedText: Ta hanyar jagorantar yakin 'yancin kai na Turkiyya zuwa ga nasara, kawar da masarautar Sultanate, da kafa jamhuriya. Daga nan na aiwatar da sauye-sauye masu tsattsauran ra'ayi don samar da kasa mai zaman kanta ta zamani.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Navigating the decline of the Ottoman Empire while fending off the imperial ambitions of European powers like Britain and France.
translatedText: Kewaya tabarbarewar Daular Ottoman tare da kare muradun daular Turawa kamar Burtaniya da Faransa.

sourceText: By building a formidable, European-style army and navy, centralizing the state, and monopolizing trade, I created a powerful, independent base in Egypt.
translatedText: Ta hanyar gina ƙaƙƙarfan sojoji da na ruwa irin na Turai, da naɗa ƙasa, da sarrafa kasuwanci, na samar da tushe mai ƙarfi, mai zaman kansa a Masar.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Muhammad Abduh
translatedText: Muhammad Abduh

sourceText: The stagnation of Islamic societies, the dogmatism of traditionalist scholars, and the overwhelming pressure of European colonialism.
translatedText: Tabarbarewar al'ummomin Musulunci, da akidar malaman gargajiya, da kuma tsananin matsin da Turawa suka yi wa mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: Championing the use of reason (ijtihad) to reinterpret Islamic texts for contemporary times and advocating for widespread educational reform to revitalize the Muslim world.
translatedText: Jagoran yin amfani da hankali (ijtihadi) wajen sake tafsirin nassosin Musulunci a wannan zamani da bayar da shawarwarin kawo sauyi mai yawa na ilimi don farfado da duniyar musulmi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Mimar Sinan
translatedText: Mimar Sinan

sourceText: The sorrow of being taken from my family at a young age under the Devşirme system to serve as a soldier in a foreign land.
translatedText: Bakin cikin an ɗauke ni daga iyalina tun ina ƙarami a ƙarƙashin tsarin Devşirme don yin aikin soja a ƙasar waje.

sourceText: By honing my engineering and architectural skills during military service, I caught the Sultan's attention and was appointed Chief Imperial Architect, allowing me to fully realize my talents.
translatedText: Ta hanyar haɓaka fasahar injiniya da gine-gine a lokacin aikin soja, na ɗauki hankalin Sarkin Musulmi kuma aka naɗa ni Babban Masanin Gine-gine na Imperial, wanda ya ba ni damar fahimtar basirata.

sourceText: 16th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Daular Usmaniyya ta Karni na 16

sourceText: Badi' al-Zaman al-Hamadani
translatedText: Badi' al-Zaman al-Hamadani

sourceText: Constant travel and rivalry with other scholars and poets to secure patronage.
translatedText: Ci gaba da tafiye-tafiye da fafatawa tare da sauran malamai da mawaƙa don tabbatar da ikon yin biyayya.

sourceText: His unparalleled linguistic skill and the invention of the Maqama secured his fame and influence for centuries.
translatedText: Ƙwarewarsa na harshe mara misaltuwa da ƙirƙirar Maqama sun tabbatar da shahararsa da tasirinsa tsawon ƙarni.

sourceText: 10th-11th Century / Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Karni na 10-11/ Zamanin Zinare na Musulunci

sourceText: Bediüzzaman Said Nursi
translatedText: Bediuzzaman Said Nursi

sourceText: Decades of exile, imprisonment, and persecution under the secular Turkish Republic for his religious teachings.
translatedText: Shekaru goma na gudun hijira, dauri, da kuma tsanantawa a ƙarƙashin jamhuriyar Turkiyya wadda ba ruwanta da addini saboda koyarwarsa ta addini.

sourceText: He transformed his suffering into a spiritual victory by writing the 6,000-page Risale-i Nur, which created a powerful spiritual revival.
translatedText: Ya canza wahalarsa zuwa nasara ta ruhaniya ta hanyar rubuta shafi 6,000 Risale-i Nur, wanda ya haifar da farkawa ta ruhaniya mai ƙarfi.
